§6Bedrock Ore Processor§f

§aThe first step in processing raw bedrock ore — split it into six categories of refined ore types!§f

The §bBedrock Ore Processor§f (also referred to as the 'ore slopper') is the first machine in the bedrock ore processing chain. It accepts raw bedrock ore from the Large Mining Drill through any of its fluid ports and refines it into six different types of bedrock ore: §bLight Metal§f, §bHeavy Metal§f, §bRare Earth§f, §bActinide§f, §bNon-Metal§f, and §bCrystalline§f. The proportions depend on the vein's composition and any effectiveness upgrades installed.

§6Crafting§f (Assembly Machine):
§b• §e6×§f Cast Steel Plate
§b• §e8×§f Titanium Plate
§b• §e3×§f Copper Pipe
§b• §e3×§f Motor
§b• §e1×§f Analog Circuit Board

§6Operation§f:
§b• Input§f: Raw Bedrock Ore (via fluid ports — the machine treats ore as a 'fluid').
§b• Power§f: Constant electrical draw during operation.
§b• Fluid§f: Requires water to function.
§b• Output§f: Six categorized ore types, plus §bOre Slop§f as byproduct.

§6Byproduct — Ore Slop§f:
Ore Slop can be fed into an §bElectrolysis Machine§f to produce §bMercury§f, §bNiter§f, §bLimestone Dust§f, and §bSulfur§f — turning waste into valuable materials.

§eTip§f: The Bedrock Ore Processor is the gateway to infinite resources. Route its outputs directly into a §bCentrifuge§f for initial processing, then use the §bOre Acidizer§f with different solvents to maximize yields!
